page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg9i.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor running at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance utilizes the KVM hypervisor. The CPU configuration features 4 physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading.

The instance does not include local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for data persistence. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offer a balanced resource density for workloads requiring predictable CPU performance without the overhead of shared-core environments.

Given its balanced memory-to-CPU ratio and dedicated Intel Xeon cores, the ecs.hfg9i.xlarge is suited for general-purpose workloads, medium-traffic web servers, application servers, and containerized microservices that require consistent compute performance.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg9i.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor, it is powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The CPU configuration provides 4 physical cores with a single thread per core, ensuring dedicated execution resources. This instance does not include local storage, relying instead on network-attached storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, it offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring stable, non-burstable compute performance. It is suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and microservices.

tagline: A dedicated four-core x86_64 virtual server with a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio for predictable general-purpose compute workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs at 3.4 GHz clock speed
16.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external connectivity
KVM hypervisor virtualization with zero local storage included
Suited for general-purpose applications, web servers, and microservices
